title: Convolvulus beladambu
taxonomy: Convolvulus beladambu is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Convolvulaceae, commonly known as the morning glory family. This family consists of about 60 genera and over 1,600 species of flowering plants, many of which are climbers or twiners. The genus Convolvulus is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world, and it is characterized by its trumpet-shaped flowers and twining stems. Convolvulus beladambu is a species native to India, and it is often found growing in dry, rocky areas. Further classification of Convolvulus beladambu may include its classification as a member of the section or series within the genus Convolvulus.

intro: Convolvulus beladambu, also known as Beladambu, is a plant species belonging to the Convolvulaceae family, commonly used in traditional Indian medicine. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Convolvulus beladambu include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Convolvulus beladambu is often used to prepare herbal decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are commonly used to treat fever, digestive problems, and skin infections.
